rulings.Case Title: TONY FU VS. DEMAS YAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 30, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFF TONY FU'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION AND TO VOID ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT KAMAN LIU'S MOTION FOR MANDATORY DISMISSAL OF PLAINTIFF'S ACTION PURSUANT TO CCP 1008 is DENIED. Moving party fails to sustain their burden under CCP 1008. However, even if the arguments regarding tolling (arguments that could have been, but were not made previously) were considered, Plaintiff failed to show that 5 years would not have run before October 7, 2022, the date of the original hearing. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Defendant Private Investment Equity Llc To Produce All Documents Responsive To Plaintiffs Requests For Documents, Set One, Numbers 12 And 18; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Private Investment Equity Llc And Its Attorney Of Record, Andrew Werner, In The Amount Of $6,822.00

rulings.Case Title: THEODORE SCOTT-SMITH ET AL VS. IGOR UNGER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 30, 2022 line 3. DEFENDANTS IGOR UNGER AND LIDIYA SKLOVSKAYA'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT is DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain their burden under CCP 473(b) or CCP 473.5. Default was entered on April 7, 2021 (more than 6 months before the filing of this motion); Notice of Entry of Judgment was served on May 20, 2022 (more than 120 days before the filing of this motion); reference to relief under CCP 473(d) is not supported by argument or evidence presented with the moving papers. Additionally, Defendant fails to sustain their burden to show lack of actual notice of the lawsuit, mistake, surprise or excusable neglect. The Court will not consider new arguments and evidence submitted in reply. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LORAN SIMON VS. TODD BRABEC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 30, 2022 line 7. DEFENDANTS TODD BRABEC, NANCY GOLDSEN AND DEBORAH ROSENTHAL'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the first, second, sixteenth causes and seventeenth of action for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions, in support of each element of each cause of action. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the thirteenth cause of action; no opposition filed. Demurrer to the causes of action for public nuisance, intentional misrepresentation, fraudulent concealment, negligence, breach of contact (common areas breach of CC&Rs/failure to enforce, slander of title, interference with contractual relations is overruled. All elements are sufficiently alleged. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
